hook: Sending a SoundCloud link to a music supervisor is like sending a screenshot of your CV to a hiring manager.

slides.eyebrow: Education · Why your SoundCloud link isn\
slides.headline: Sending a SoundCloud link to a music supervisor is like sending a screenshot of
slides.lede: your CV to a hiring manager.

slides.headline: Here's what a supervisor actually needs to move your track forward:
slides.lede: It tells them you exist. That's all. ↓

slides.headline:
✓ High-quality WAV or AIFF
Not a stream.
slides.lede: Not a download link. A file, ready immediately.

slides.headline:
✓ Embedded metadata
Title, composer, ISRC, mood, genre, BPM — inside the file,

✓ Rights status confirmed
One-stop or clearly defined split, documented before
slides.lede:
you pitch. · ✓ Stems available
Not now. But ready when they ask.

slides.headline:
✓ Response within 24 hours
If you respond in 3 days, the slot is filled.
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: SoundCloud tells a supervisor: · A track exists · You have a profile · That's not enough to move a placement forward.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Why streaming links fail supervisors every time · The file format issue — and what broadcast spec actually means · What proper metadata looks like inside a file · How to handle the rights conversation before it becomes a problem · Save this before your next outreach.
